Title: AVideo has Stored XSS via Unescaped Menu Item Fields in TopMenu Plugin
Description: The TopMenu plugin renders menu item fields (icon classes, URLs, and text labels) directly into HTML without applying `htmlspecialchars()` or any other output encoding. Since menu items are rendered on every public page through plugin hooks, a single malicious menu entry results in stored cross-site scripting that executes for every visitor to the site. An admin user who is tricked into saving a crafted menu item (or an attacker who gains admin access) can compromise all site visitors.
Solution: Unfortunately, there is no solution available yet.
